---
title: "Welcome"
---


Welcome to **R Fundamentals for Health Research** — an introduction to R for epidemiology and health policy research.

These course materials run entirely in your browser using [Quarto](https://quarto.org) and [WebR](https://docs.r-wasm.org/webr/latest/). You do not need R installed locally to explore the tutorials and run code on this site.

```{webr-r}
#| context: setup
# Helper: download bundled data into the WebR virtual filesystem
webr_read_csv <- function(path) {
  local_name <- basename(path)
  if (!file.exists(local_name)) {
    download.file(path, local_name, quiet = TRUE, mode = "wb")
  }
  readr::read_csv(local_name, show_col_types = FALSE)
}
```

Try loading a course dataset:

```{webr-r}
framingham <- webr_read_csv("data/framingham.csv")
nrow(framingham)
```

## How to use this site

- **Tutorials** — Pre-class activities with interactive code cells. Click **Run** in any code block to execute R in your browser.
- **Homework** — Assignments with editable code cells. Download the original `.Rmd` from the course repo if you prefer working in RStudio.
- **Final Project** — End-of-quarter analysis assignment.

::: {.callout-note}
The first time you run code on a page, WebR needs to download R and any required packages. This can take 30–60 seconds on a slow connection. Subsequent cells on the same page run much faster.
:::

## Working locally in RStudio

If you prefer the full RStudio experience, clone the [course repository](https://github.com/r-pophealth-research/online-course) and open the `.Rmd` files directly. The `.qmd` files on this site are generated from those sources for WebR compatibility.
